comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
**Audit PR Docs**: Inaccurate claim detected.

Claim: `extract_leading_issue_number()` exists but "Always returns `None`"
Reality: This function has been completely removed from `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/naming.py` — it does not exist in the codebase.

Source: `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/naming.py`

Suggested fix: Remove the entire `extract_leading_issue_number()` subsection (lines 62–66), including the source pointer. The function no longer exists, so documenting it (even as deprecated) creates confusion about available symbols.

comments.author: schrockn
comments.body:
Already fixed: the extract_leading_issue_number() subsection has been removed from branch-naming.md — the section no longer exists in the current version.

_Addressed via `/erk:pr-address` at 2026-02-24 17:45 PST_

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
**Audit PR Docs**: Drift risk — documents a private method name that will change.

Source: `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/plan_data_provider/real.py, _build_worktree_mapping`

Risk: Private `_underscore` methods are the most volatile identifiers in a codebase. Per learned-docs-core.md, they must never appear in documentation — their behavior belongs in docstrings, not learned docs. Consider replacing the three-bullet list with a single sentence: "Plan-to-branch mapping reads `.impl/plan-ref.json` from each worktree — see `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/plan_data_provider/real.py`."

comments.author: schrockn
comments.body:
Already fixed: the private method _build_worktree_mapping() reference has been replaced — no such reference exists in the current branch-naming.md.

_Addressed via `/erk:pr-address` at 2026-02-24 17:45 PST_

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
**Audit PR Docs**: Inaccurate claim detected.

Claim: "All branch creation codepaths use `generate_planned_pr_branch_name()`" — table lists `submit.py` and `setup-impl-from-issue`
Reality: Neither file calls `generate_planned_pr_branch_name()`. `submit.py` uses an existing PR's branch (no branch generation). `setup_impl_from_issue.py` gets the branch via `pr_result.head_ref_name` at line 165.

Source: `src/erk/cli/commands/submit.py`, `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/setup_impl_from_issue.py:165`

Suggested fix: Remove the `submit.py` and `setup-impl-from-issue` rows from the Usage Sites table. The only codepaths that actually call `generate_planned_pr_branch_name()` are `exec/scripts/plan_save.py` and `one_shot_dispatch.py`.

comments.author: schrockn
comments.body:
Already fixed: submit.py and setup-impl-from-issue have been removed from the Usage Sites table — it now only contains plan_save.py and one_shot_dispatch.py.

_Addressed via `/erk:pr-address` at 2026-02-24 17:45 PST_

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
**Audit PR Docs**: Inaccurate claim detected.

Claim: "GitHubPlanStore (legacy)" path exists where `create_plan_issue()` is called first, then a branch and PR are created.
Reality: `src/erk/cli/commands/one_shot_dispatch.py` has NO backend-conditional branching. It always uses the PlannedPRBackend path — `generate_planned_pr_branch_name()` is called unconditionally. There is no `create_plan_issue()` call in this file.

Source: `src/erk/cli/commands/one_shot_dispatch.py:171-296`

Suggested fix: Remove the "GitHubPlanStore (legacy)" subsection (lines 59-76). The dispatch function has a single code path. If the legacy path needs documentation for historical context, add a brief note that the GitHubPlanStore backend path was removed, but do not describe it as a still-present code path.

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body: **Dignified Code Simplifier Review**: Unnecessary conditional expression -  handles non-existent paths internally, so the ternary can be simplified to just . Reference: dignified-python LBYL pattern.

comments.author: schrockn
comments.body:
Already fixed: the unnecessary conditional/ternary around read_plan_ref() in impl_folder.py has been simplified — no ternary exists in the current code.

_Addressed via `/erk:pr-address` at 2026-02-24 17:45 PST_
